title: Project-Oriented Conversational Operator
summary: Completed the operator-foundation slice: user-owned projects and project items, agent-to-project association, operator events and subscriptions, durable todos, user memory, project-aware conversational context, itemized Telegram todo delivery, and a dashboard for projects, todos, memories, connected accounts, and agent activity.
next_action: Implement the follow-on spec for recommendation acceptance, repo grants, coding runs, and PR lifecycle.

verification.notes: The re-scoped foundation slice now matches what is actually shipped, and the repo passed `mix precommit` with `987 tests, 0 failures` on 2026-04-02. Recommendation acceptance, repo access grants, and PR automation were split into a separate ready spec so they can be implemented as one coherent next-stage workflow.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Closed the broad operator spec as a shipped foundation slice and spun the recommendation-to-PR loop into its own ready spec.
last_reviewed_sections: 13. Definition of Done For This Foundation Slice
resume_context.notes: The user-facing decision is now explicit: Maraithon already has the conversational operator foundation and should stop pretending the still-missing recommendation-to-PR workflow is part of the same unfinished slice.

history.note: Created a new spec for the project-oriented conversational operator model built on connected accounts, specialist agents, projects, and one natural-language assistant.
Started Phase 1 implementation for project primitives, project-aware agent association, and conversational context exposure.
Split the overbroad v2 draft into a shipped operator foundation spec plus a dedicated follow-on spec for recommendation acceptance, repo grants, coding runs, and PR lifecycle.
Marked the conversational operator foundation slice done after final review against the updated canonical spec and a green `mix precommit` gate.
